dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a traffic accident on the left side of the highway.
Hello, I'm John with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
My girlfriend was in a car crash. She lost consciousness briefly and has severe pain in her left hip.
Can you tell me your name, please?
What is the name of this city?
I'll take that as a normal verbal and motor response.
Your airway is clear and patent. I want to immobilize you for safety. Is that okay with you? 8. Primary Assessment; check airway: distress; Patient: Yes, go ahead. I can't move my left leg.
Respirations are 18 per minute and shallow. Let me confirm breath sounds now.
Normal bilateral breath sounds noted. 11. Primary Assessment; check breathing: Rate and depth; EMT: Stay with me. Was there any sudden onset of this pain?
Pulse is strong and regular at 78. Radials are equal and strong.
Skin is pink and dry.
Warm and dry skin.
Blood pressure is 114 over 67.
Your breathing remains regular at 18. Keep it steady as I continue the exam.
4-lead EKG placed. Sinus rhythm noted at this time.
Pulse ox reads 95%. Just making sure we've got the best reading possible.
Blood glucose was not measured at this time.
Recheck pulse at 78.
Rechecking respirations, now 20 per minute.

Your pain is 10. It's sharp and throbbing, severely limiting leg movement. 23. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: events leading up to the event; EMT: Her boyfriend reported he didn't see the stopped vehicle. She lost consciousness briefly. 24. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: signs and symptoms; Partner: She cannot move her left leg and expresses severe left hip pain.
No known drug allergy or prior allergic reactions mentioned.
She is currently in a methadone maintenance program. Any other prescriptions you're on?

She used heroin for 6 years and has been sober for 3 years. She has been in a methadone program for the past year. 28. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: last oral intake; Partner: She did not mention any recent food or drink intake.
She is immobilized with full spinal restriction now-backboard and cervical collar in place.
4-lead EKG continues for monitoring. 31. Take Interventions; take procedures: IV Start - extremity vein (arm or leg); Partner: I've placed a 20G IV in the right arm after two attempts.
Rechecking EKG-still shows sinus rhythm.
Vitals remain stable-77 HR, 20 RR, 102/71 BP, 99% O2 saturation.
She presents with left hip pain and a suspected injury. Proceeding under general trauma management, protocol 4-1.
Transport is in progress to [COMPANY_NAME] for trauma care.
She is secure on a backboard and stretcher. All straps and restraints applied.
Stay still as we transfer you to the ambulance. We'll be at the hospital shortly.
Calling ahead to [COMPANY_NAME]: 34-year-old female with hip and left leg pain, BP 102/71, HR 77. IV in place.
You did great. Once in the ambulance, I'll keep an eye on your IV.
No significant EKG changes from the most recent reading.
She will remain immobilized during transport. All movement restricted.
No EKG changes observed from the most recent reading.
All restraints have been applied correctly. No risk for hip movement.
Pulse again at 77. Still within normal limits.
IV is being maintained. I'll double-check that in a moment.
Requesting confirmation that [COMPANY_NAME] is ready.
All motion is restricted. Straps are in place and tightened.
Initiating exit. She appears stable with IV and EKG normal.
EKG remains normal with no changes.
Just confirming restraints in place for safety. IV and EKG normal.
[COMPANY_NAME] is confirmed as the destination.
All movement is fixed. She is stable for transport.
No movement from last check. Positioning secure.
Still at 99%
EKG continues normal.
Just making sure IV is not kinked and flowing.
Ensuring that all movement is fixed-shoulders, legs, neck are stable.
Letting [COMPANY_NAME] know we're en route with a 34-year-old female with left hip and leg pain.
All set for ambulance ride in.
There is instability and crepitus noted at the hip. You also have presence of PMS distally in both legs.
She is secure and immobilized. All procedures complete.
Full Spinal Restriction
Final check-she is in place, IV running, vitals stable.
She is safely transferred. All per protocol.
Handoff next, everything is in order.
Recheck heart rate at 77.
IV continues normal flow.
EKG remains normal.
No loss of distal movement or sensation.
Patient continues stable and comfortable. Secured in transport.
Rechecking straps-no signs of loosening.
All status updated for [COMPANY_NAME].
IV line is clear, flow free, and site intact.
Still monitoring 102 over 71.
You did great. We're almost to the hospital.
All supports remain in place for hip stability.
No changes. All secure for transport.
Handing off to ER next. Everything is in order.
EKG monitoring continues with normal rhythm.
Standing at 102 over 71.

Let the hospital know anything changes. 82. Take Vital Signs; retake vital signs: RR; EMT: Respiratory rate remains at 20. 83. Take Vital Signs; retake vital signs: pulse; Partner: Heart rate unchanged and stable.
Patient is stable, no movement. All secure.
Transportation proceeding smoothly. 86. Take Vital Signs; retake vital signs: ekg; Partner: Recheck EKG noted normal rhythm.
Reiterate transport to [COMPANY_NAME] for further treatment.
No changes. All secure for the remainder.
All systems go for the remainder of the ride. 90. Take Vital Signs; retake vital signs: BP; EMT: Stable pressure-102 over 71.
All status updated for [COMPANY_NAME].
Handover to [COMPANY_NAME] is ready.
No movement during transport. Secure and stable.
Patient did not fully cooperate throughout this transport. We were not able to obtain her phone number or social security.
All secured for the rest of the ride. Discontinuing backup monitoring at this point.
